meta_title: Roofing in Lauderhill, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Lauderhill, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates — call today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair in Lauderhill, Florida
intro:
Lauderhill is a city that wears its character proudly — from the manicured fairways of Inverrary Country Club to the community energy of Central Broward Regional Park and the cultural landmark that is the Lauderhill Performing Arts Center. But behind the curb appeal of neighborhoods like Lauderhill Isles, Sunrise Golf Village, and the Lauderhill Mall Area lies a persistent challenge that every homeowner here eventually faces: an aging roof that may no longer be up to the demands of South Florida's extreme weather. With average home values sitting around $280,000 and over half of Lauderhill's housing stock built before 1994, the roof over your head is arguably the most critical investment you can make to protect your home's value and your family's safety.

Lauderhill sits approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coast, which places it squarely in a 170 mph wind zone under Broward County's building standards. While the city falls outside the official High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) designation that applies to Miami-Dade and coastal Broward, the Florida Building Code still mandates hurricane-rated roofing systems throughout this region. That means every new roof installation in Lauderhill — whether you're in ZIP code 33311, 33313, 33319, or 33351 — must meet rigorous uplift resistance requirements. Older roofs installed before these standards were adopted simply don't make the cut when the next storm rolls through.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners navigate the full roofing replacement process, from permit filing with the City of Lauderhill Building Division to final inspection sign-off. We bring more than 60 years of combined installation experience and a 100% in-house crew — no subcontractors, no surprises. Whether your shingles are curling after years of summer heat, your tile has shifted following a tropical storm, or you simply know it's time to upgrade before the next hurricane season, we're here to make the process straightforward, code-compliant, and affordable.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderhill Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Florida isn't just about price — it's about trust, expertise, and the assurance that the work will hold up when a Category 3 storm is bearing down on Broward County. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by consistently delivering installations that exceed code requirements and stand the test of time. Our A+ BBB rating and 500+ completed roofing projects across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties speak for themselves.

One of the most important things that sets us apart is our 100% in-house installation model. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, every crew member who sets foot on your Lauderhill roof is a trained, background-checked Premier employee — not a day-labor subcontractor. This means quality control is maintained at every stage of the project, from tear-off and underlayment to final ridge cap installation. Our field teams are well-versed in the specific permit requirements enforced by the City of Lauderhill Building Division, so you won't face delays or costly re-inspections due to installation errors.

We understand the financial reality facing many Lauderhill homeowners. With home values averaging around $280,000 and the city serving a predominantly lower-middle income community, we've made it a priority to offer accessible financing options — including plans starting at just $87 per month with $0 down. A new roof is not a luxury; it's a necessity, and it should be within reach for every homeowner regardless of their current financial situation. Our team will walk you through every financing option and help you understand exactly what your investment will look like month to month.

With over 60 years of combined experience across our team, we've seen what South Florida storms do to under-performing roofing systems. We've worked in virtually every neighborhood in Lauderhill, from the older ranch-style homes near Lauderhill Isles to the larger single-family properties in Inverrary. That local familiarity means we come to every project prepared — knowing the common decking conditions, the roof pitches, and the code nuances that make Broward County roofing work unique.

section_history.h2: Lauderhill's Storm History: Why a Hurricane-Rated Roof Is Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Lauderhill may not sit on the beachfront, but it has endured more than its share of hurricane punishment. Since 1990, the city has been impacted by nine named storms, with three standing out as particularly destructive. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for South Florida roofing — it exposed catastrophic weaknesses in pre-code construction and led directly to the sweeping changes in the Florida Building Code that followed. Many Lauderhill homes built before 1994 survived Andrew with significant damage, and those that weren't properly repaired or upgraded have continued to deteriorate ever since.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a different kind of devastation to Broward County. As one of the fastest-intensifying Atlantic hurricanes on record, Wilma tracked directly across South Florida, stripping shingles, displacing tile, and exposing attic spaces to torrential rainfall across thousands of homes in Lauderhill and neighboring cities like Fort Lauderdale, Sunrise, and Plantation. The storm reinforced what contractors and code officials had been saying for years: roofing systems that weren't anchored with modern hurricane-rated fasteners and high-wind underlayments were a liability. Hurricane Irma in 2017, while not as directly catastrophic as Wilma, produced sustained wind gusts across Broward County that caused widespread shingle loss, particularly on roofs that were already aging or improperly installed.

For the 55% of Lauderhill homes built before 1994, this history isn't abstract — it's a blueprint for what can happen when an older roof meets a modern storm. Upgrading to a properly installed, hurricane-rated roofing system isn't just about weathering the next big one; it's about protecting your equity, your belongings, and the safety of everyone inside your home when skies turn dark.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance for Roofing in Lauderhill and Broward County
section_code.body:
Understanding roofing code requirements in Lauderhill is essential before any replacement project begins. The city falls under Broward County's jurisdiction within the Florida Building Code (FBC), and while Lauderhill is not designated as a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a status reserved for Miami-Dade County and coastal Broward — it still operates under demanding wind-resistance standards appropriate for its 170 mph wind zone designation. These aren't suggestions; they are legally enforceable requirements that must be met for any permitted roof installation.

All roofing work in Lauderhill requires a permit pulled through the City of Lauderhill Building Division. This process involves submittal of product approval documentation showing that all materials — including shingles, tile, underlayment, and fasteners — have been tested and approved for use in Florida's wind environment. Products must carry a Florida Product Approval Number, and the installation method must follow the manufacturer's specifications exactly as approved. Inspections are required at multiple stages of the project, including a mid-roof inspection to verify decking conditions and underlayment before the final covering is applied, and a final inspection once the roof is complete.

For homes in Lauderhill's 33311, 33313, 33319, and 33351 ZIP codes, the code also dictates specific requirements around secondary water barriers — a protective underlayment layer designed to keep water out if the primary roofing surface is compromised during a storm. Additionally, re-nailing of the roof deck is commonly required during replacement projects when the existing sheathing doesn't meet current spacing and fastener standards. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of this permitting and inspection process on your behalf, ensuring your new roof is fully code-compliant from day one.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Built for Lauderhill's Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted roofing product lines in South Florida: CertainTeed architectural shingles — specifically the renowned Landmark series — and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines are selected for their proven performance in high-wind environments and their compatibility with Florida Building Code requirements, making them ideal choices for Lauderhill homeowners looking to upgrade from older, underperforming roofs.

The CertainTeed Landmark series is one of the most popular architectural shingle systems in the country, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles are engineered with a dual-layered construction that provides superior wind resistance and a distinctive shadow-line appearance that dramatically improves curb appeal. CertainTeed Landmark shingles carry a Class A fire rating and are warranted against wind damage at speeds consistent with South Florida's exposure levels. For Lauderhill homeowners in neighborhoods like Sunrise Golf Village or the Lauderhill Mall Area, where traditional shingle roofs are common, the Landmark series offers an attractive, cost-effective upgrade that doesn't sacrifice performance for aesthetics.

For homeowners who prefer the classic look of tile — especially common throughout Inverrary and Lauderhill Isles — West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer an unmatched combination of beauty, longevity, and wind resistance. West Lake tile products carry Florida Product Approvals for high-wind installation and are designed to withstand the sustained pressure and uplift forces generated by major hurricanes. Concrete tile offers exceptional durability and thermal mass, helping to regulate attic temperatures and reduce cooling costs during Lauderhill's long, hot summers. Clay tile, prized for its natural aesthetic and lighter weight, provides a slightly different profile that complements Mediterranean and Spanish-style architecture. Both options are installed by our in-house crew using code-compliant fastening systems that ensure each tile stays firmly in place, even under extreme wind loads.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Costs in Lauderhill: What to Expect and How to Finance Your Replacement
section_cost.body:
Roof replacement is one of the most significant home improvement investments a Lauderhill homeowner can make, and understanding what it costs — and what it returns — is essential to making an informed decision. For a typical single-family home in Lauderhill, a full roof replacement using CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingles generally ranges from $12,000 to $18,000 depending on the size of the home, the complexity of the roof geometry, and any decking repairs needed. West Lake tile systems, given the additional labor and material costs associated with tile installation, typically range from $18,000 to $28,000 or more for larger homes in neighborhoods like Inverrary.

With average home values in Lauderhill sitting at approximately $280,000, a new hurricane-rated roof can represent an immediate boost to your property's market value — industry estimates consistently show that a new roof adds significant resale value and can be a decisive factor in buyer negotiations. Beyond resale, the return on investment in the form of reduced homeowner's insurance premiums can be substantial. Broward County homeowners with verified wind mitigation features on their roof — particularly newer, code-compliant installations — routinely see annual insurance savings of $800 to $2,500 or more, depending on their carrier and current coverage level.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes new roofing accessible for every Lauderhill household through flexible financing options designed with lower-middle income homeowners in mind. Our financing programs start at just $87 per month with $0 down, allowing you to move forward with a full roof replacement without draining your savings. Our team will help you evaluate the total cost of ownership — factoring in insurance savings, increased home value, and reduced maintenance costs — so you can see clearly why waiting to replace an aging roof almost always costs more in the long run.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Lauderhill Roof Replacements
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for replacing an aging Lauderhill roof isn't just about storm protection — it's about what a new, code-compliant roof can do for your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has become increasingly volatile, and Broward County homeowners are particularly vulnerable to premium increases and non-renewals when carriers assess the age and condition of a roof. Homes with roofs that are 15 years old or older are routinely flagged by insurers, and some carriers are now refusing to renew policies — or writing new ones — unless the roof meets certain age and construction standards.

A new hurricane-rated roof triggers eligibility for a Wind Mitigation Inspection, a state-authorized assessment that documents the specific storm-resistant features of your roof system. Key credits are available for roofs with a hip geometry, secondary water barriers, verified nail spacing on the roof deck, and products rated for the wind loads applicable to Lauderhill's 170 mph zone. When these credits are applied by your insurance carrier — which may include Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, or other major Florida writers — the resulting discounts can be significant. Many Lauderhill homeowners report annual premium reductions of $1,000 to $2,500 after a new compliant roof and subsequent wind mitigation inspection.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing works directly with homeowners and their insurance agents to ensure the documentation needed for wind mitigation credits is accurate and complete. Our installations are designed from the start to maximize the credits available under Florida's mitigation program. We can also help homeowners who've experienced storm damage understand the claims process and coordinate with their adjuster to ensure the full scope of covered damage is properly identified and included in the claim. From the permit stage through the wind mitigation report, we're with you every step of the way.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Lauderhill home needs a full roof replacement or just a repair?
faqs.a: If your roof is more than 15–20 years old, shows widespread shingle granule loss, has multiple leak points, or was installed before the 1994 Florida Building Code updates, a full replacement is almost always the more cost-effective long-term solution. Localized damage from a single storm event, such as a few missing shingles, may qualify for a repair. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections to assess your specific situation and give you an honest recommendation based on the actual condition of your roof.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Lauderhill, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements in Lauderhill range from $12,000 to $28,000 depending on roof size, material choice, and deck conditions. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it accessible for homeowners across all income levels. Our team will provide a detailed written estimate so you know exactly what to expect before any work begins, with no hidden fees or surprise charges.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Lauderhill, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all full roof replacements and many significant repairs in Lauderhill. The permit must be filed with the City of Lauderhill Building Division, and all materials must carry a Florida Product Approval Number. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission and documentation to scheduling required inspections. You won't have to navigate the permit office yourself; we take care of it as part of every project.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical home in Lauderhill?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Lauderhill, a shingle roof replacement can be completed in one to two days once materials are on-site and the permit is active. Tile roofs typically take two to four days due to the additional complexity of the installation. Permit processing through the City of Lauderhill Building Division generally adds five to fifteen business days before physical work can begin, though timing can vary. We'll keep you informed throughout the entire process with clear communication at every stage.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowner's insurance in Lauderhill?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof makes your home eligible for a Wind Mitigation Inspection, which can unlock credits for features like secondary water barriers, reinforced deck attachment, and high-wind-rated materials. Broward County homeowners with qualifying roofs commonly see annual insurance savings ranging from $1,000 to $2,500 depending on their carrier and coverage level. These savings can offset a substantial portion of the cost of your new roof over time, making replacement one of the highest-ROI improvements available to Lauderhill homeowners.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Lauderhill Home with a New Hurricane-Rated Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof inspection and estimate — our in-house team is ready to get your Lauderhill home storm-ready before the next hurricane season arrives.
